channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: USDA launches review at University of Maine over transgender sports policy after Trump-Gov. Mills blowup
item.description: Federal agencies launched reviews into the University of Maine's Title IX policies after a tense exchange between President Trump and the state's governor.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump revels in mass federal firings and jeers at Biden before adoring conservative crowd
item.description: President Donald Trump says “nobody has ever seen anything” like his administration’s sweeping effort to fire thousands of federal employees and shrink the size of government.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: At CPAC, Argentina’s Milei explains his chainsaw methods and likens them to Musk’s DOGE
item.description: OXON HILL, Md. (AP) — Argentina’s President Javier Milei on Saturday urged an enthusiastic crowd of U.S. conservatives to keep supporting measures to shrink the size of their government, saying his methods are similar to those undertaken by President Donald Trump and close ally Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin Democratic governor proposes replacing 'mother' with 'inseminated person' in state law
item.description: Wisconsin Gov. Tony Evers is taking heat for introducing a bill with several changes to gendered language, including using the term "inseminated person" instead of "mother.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meet four Minnesota federal workers mourning the loss of their dream jobs
item.description: Many Minnesotans are among those facing unemployment, as the Trump administration slashes jobs across agencies. The state is home to more than 18,000 federal workers, with nearly 60 percent working for the Department of Veterans Affairs and the U.S. Department of Agriculture.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Nonprofit working on Mississippi River project loses federal funding
item.description: Mississippi Park Connection said funds for restoration work that has not yet been completed are frozen, as are funds for congressionally funded work that has already been completed.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Trump moves with light speed and brute force in shaking the core of what America has been
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is moving with light speed and brute force to break the existing order and reshape America at home and abroad. He likes the ring of calling himself king. No one can absorb it all.
item.dc:creator: CALVIN WOODWARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NRSC chair reveals how many GOP Senate seats he's gunning for during 2026 midterms
item.description: National Republican Senatorial Committee Chair Tim Scott aims to expand the GOP's majority in the chamber in the 2026 midterm elections and thanks President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-Proud Boys leader Enrique Tarrio arrested near Capitol
item.description: Enrique Tarrio, the former national chairman of the Proud Boys, was arrested by U.S. Capitol Police on Friday following an altercation with a counter protester.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: After years of firm support, 10 days upended the US approach to Ukraine
item.description: As Ukraine approached the three-year mark of Russia’s full-scale invasion, the country’s hoped-for path to a favorable and lasting peace has been upended in a matter of days by the administration of U.S. President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Michigan state lawmaker beats out loyal Trump ally charged in fake elector case for GOP party chair
item.description: Michigan Republicans have elected a state lawmaker to lead the GOP into the 2026 midterms, State Sen.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Volmert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DOJ issues complaint about federal judge’s ‘misconduct’ while presiding over military trans ban court case
item.description: The Department of Justice issued a complaint about the alleged "misconduct" of U.S. District Judge Ana Reyes while presiding over a case about trans people's service in the military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump fires top US general in unprecedented Pentagon shakeup
item.description: A U.S. official said Brown was relieved with immediate effect, even before the Senate confirms his successor.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutors: Presidential pardon for Jan. 6 does not apply to Maryville man's FBI ‘kill list’ plot conviction
item.description: Prosecutors have responded to a motion filed by a Maryville man to dismiss his conviction in to a 2022 plot to murder federal investigators at the FBI Knoxville Field Office after he was pardoned for his role in the January 6, 2021 insurrection at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, NewsNation

item.title: Judge largely blocks Trump’s executive orders ending federal support for DEI programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday largely blocked sweeping executive orders from President Donald Trump that seek to end government support for programs promoting diversity, equity and inclusion. U.S.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge clears way for Trump administration to pull thousands of USAID staffers off the job
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday cleared the way for one of the Trump administration's remaining steps in its dismantling of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Supreme Court won’t allow Trump to immediately fire head of whistleblower office
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday temporarily kept on the job the head of the federal agency that protects government whistleblowers, in its first word on the many legal fights over President Donald Trump’s second-term agenda.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Dexter's Law: Pushing for tougher sentencing for animal cruelty
item.description: One Tampa Bay area lawmaker is pushing to toughen sentences for animal cruelty, specifically in cases like the shocking discovery of Dexter, a decapitated dog found at Fort DeSoto Beach.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump spars with Maine's governor at the White House over transgender athletes
item.description: President Donald Trump sparred with Maine's Democratic governor during a meeting of governors at the White House on Friday, with Gov. Janet Mills telling the Republican president, “We'll see you in court,” over his push to deny federal funding to the state over transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: ‘In Trump We Trust’ Is Exploding
item.description: President Trump forced through his Cabinet – we told you that would only increase his control over the Republican Party, and it did. To that point, Matt Lewis headlines in The Hill, “Conservatism used to mean something. Now it’s just whatever Trump says.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: New FBI Director Kash Patel plans to move up to 1,500 workers out of Washington, AP source says
item.description: New FBI Director Kash Patel has told senior officials that he plans to relocate 1,000 employees from Washington to field offices around the country and move an additional 500 to a bureau facility in Huntsville, Alabama.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Justice Department files complaint against judge weighing challenge to Trump’s transgender troop ban
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department filed a complaint Friday accusing a federal judge in Washington of misconduct during hearings over President Donald Trump's executive order that calls for banning transgender troops from serving in the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-Proud Boys leader Enrique Tarrio arrested near Capitol on assault charge after press conference
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former Proud Boys national leader Enrique Tarrio was arrested Friday near the U.S. Capitol on a charge that he assaulted a woman protesting a gathering attended by Tarrio and others who received presidential pardons for crimes stemming from the Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration reassigns acting ICE director
item.description: The Trump administration is replacing the acting head of Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) amid simmering from some officials about deportation numbers. A Department of Homeland Security official confirmed that Caleb Vitello was being reassigned within the agency.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate adopts budget resolution after marathon overnight vote-a-rama
item.description: The Senate adopted a budget resolution Friday intended to serve as a blueprint to deliver the first part of President Trump’s agenda. Senators voted 52-48, mostly along party lines, on the resolution after a marathon overnight voting session. Sen. Rand Paul (R-Ky.) voted against the measure.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: That marks a break from President Trump and tech billionaire and Trump adviser Elon Musk, who have floated the idea in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump moves hamper bird flu response as egg prices spike
item.description: When President Trump took office, his administration instituted an external communications blackout across health agencies.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: A gold-framed Trump mug shot is hanging just outside the Oval Office
item.description: President Trump’s mug shot is one of the first things visitors will see when approaching the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise, MPR News

item.title: Latino business owners say customers are staying away in wake of deportation threats
item.description: Some Latino business owners say customers have been staying away out of fear of being detained and deported. Twin Cities business and city leaders are encouraging people to buy from Latino-owned shops this Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina, MPR News

item.title: Florida Democrats file bill to repeal 6-week abortion ban
item.description: House Minority Leader Fentrice Driskell, who represents families in Hillsborough County, said the legislation comes because it’s a mandate from voters.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5
